behavior_summary: This script implements the runtime behavior for a "child" NPC: inspect (U8P_CHILD::look), reaction to being hit/affected (U8P_CHILD::cast), player interaction/dialogue (U8P_CHILD::use), and cleanup when leaving a fast-area (U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea).
U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea is a short cleanup process that clears several conversation/activity reference slots so the NPC stops any active conversation or interaction state.
U8P_CHILD::look provides a short description when the player examines the child; it distinguishes dead vs alive and emits a single short bark before suspending.

U8P_CHILD::cast handles the NPC being affected (e.g., attacked or spell). It uses a short-lived local counter to retry spawning a helper/timer, can spawn an auxiliary process, and alerts guards via CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime when an aggressor is detected. If alive, it emits frightened cries chosen probabilistically.

U8P_CHILD::use runs the interactive dialogue when the player talks to the child: it constructs a self-identification string, clears bookkeeping refs, optionally spawns a helper/timer, begins a conversation, and implements a menu-driven conversation loop with branching and RNG-varied openings. Conversations can end and set NPC activity state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea: immediate sequence of FREE::resetRef calls (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) then end — acts as deterministic cleanup on area-exit.
U8P_CHILD::look: simple If (Npc::isDead) -> play single bark -> suspend; two short branches (dead vs alive).

U8P_CHILD::cast: initializes a small counter (local1 = 0x19), performs a spawn-and-suspend retry loop while no target exists (decrementing local1 each cycle), and on detecting an aggressor (Npc::getTarget == 0x01) calls guard alert logic and spawns a longer timer; bark choice uses urandom to select between two frightened lines.

U8P_CHILD::use: constructs local2 = "I am " + getName() + ".", resets conversation refs, chooses an opening line via urandom(0x64) threshold, BeginConversation then enters a ConversationLoop where local3 (menu array) and local1 (menu choice) drive nested Ask/SuspendAssign + strcmp branching; conversation end calls EndConversation and often schedules Npc::cSetActivity.
open_questions: Exact runtime semantics and side effects of FREE::func1C19 (spawn) — appears to be a timer/auxiliary process but its concrete behavior and parameters are unclear.
Precise meanings of the various resetRef reference IDs (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) beyond 'conversation/activity bookkeeping' — their mapping to engine subsystems is inferred but not confirmed.
Whether U8P_CHILD::cast is triggered by specific damage types, spells, or generic 'affect' events (metadata shows parameter uword but semantics of that param are not fully known).
Any persistent global flags or quest state this NPC might set/read elsewhere (this script shows no global flag reads/writes but higher-level interactions may exist).

main_function.name: U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: Clear conversation and activity bookkeeping for the NPC when it leaves a fast-area trigger so the NPC stops/abandons any active conversation or interaction state.
entry_conditions: Triggered automatically when the actor/NPC exits a fast-area/trigger region (area-exit event).

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_CHILD::look handler; choose short bark depending on Npc::isDead

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::cast
function_map.role: monologue / reaction-to-affect handler; spawn retry loop, guard alert, frightened barks

function_map.name: U8P_CHILD::use
function_map.role: dialogue/interaction handler; constructs name string, resets refs, runs menu-driven conversation loop with RNG variation

function_map.name: CALGUARD::guardRespondToCrime
function_map.role: notify/trigger guard AI in response to crime/attack

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: spawn helper/timer process (appears to schedule auxiliary behavior)

external_calls.purpose: Spawn an auxiliary/timer process during retry loops or after detecting an aggressor (appears to schedule delayed behavior).
Alert guards / trigger guard AI when the NPC detects an attacker as its target.
Clear specific conversation/activity reference slots (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) to abort or reset interaction state on area exit.
Clear conversation/activity bookkeeping before starting/continuing a dialogue (observed for 0x0205 and 0x0200).
Optionally spawn an auxiliary/timer process during conversation setup (exact role of the spawn is unclear).
Set or reset NPC activity/state (e.g., return to idle) when conversation ends.
Query NPC death state to pick appropriate U8P_CHILD::look/bark text.
Check for an aggressor/attacker target to decide whether to alert guards.
Obtain NPC display name to build the self-identification menu option ("I am {name}.").
Avoid barking/reaction if the NPC is dead.
Choose between alternate frightened barks probabilistically.
Vary opening lines and some dialogue branches probabilistically.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the runtime behavior for a "child" NPC: inspect (U8P_CHILD::look), reaction to being hit/affected (U8P_CHILD::cast), player interaction/dialogue (U8P_CHILD::use), and cleanup when leaving a fast-area (U8P_CHILD::leaveFastArea).
